Known for his sharp analytical skills and strategic problem-solving abilities, Yesu has a proven track record of delivering tailored financial solutions that drive organizational growth and long-term sustainability. His expertise lies in blending technical finance acumen with business leadership to tackle complex challenges, enabling organizations to thrive in dynamic and often volatile markets. Over the course of his career, Yesu has successfully led high-impact investments and conducted in-depth feasibility studies across multiple industries, including agribusiness, construction, and financial services. His leadership has been instrumental in guiding organizations toward financial stability, operational efficiency, and scalable growth, particularly through innovative financing solutions. His strategic oversight and financial insight have not only unlocked new revenue streams but also enhanced organizational resilience in the face of economic challenges.

employment.description_of_duties: As the Project Team Leader for the Ethiopia Connect Project at AMAS, I play a pivotal role in driving strategic initiatives to enhance financial inclusion, particularly access to finance for smallholder farmers in Ethiopia. I am leading the establishment of a digital-led microfinance institution (MFI) in collaboration with both national and international consortium parties of my project by maintaining respect for cultural and individual diversities. My leadership was instrumental in raising 200% of the required capital to launch the MFI. Additionally, I manage stakeholder relationships, represent the organization in key meetings and oversee multiple high-value financial models and feasibility studies.

At Resilience BV, I spearheaded an assignment within the framework of the Ethio-Netherlands Seed Partnership project. My primary responsibility was to develop an in-depth financing strategy tailored specifically for seed-producing companies. This role entailed a thorough assessment of the financial needs and challenges faced by these companies, and devising a strategy that would enable them to access essential financial services. My approach involved identifying funding sources that would support the growth and scaling of seedling operations. By ensuring that these companies had the financial backing needed, I contributed in fostering their development and success within the agricultural sector.

As a Freelance Consultant at Precise Consult, I successfully led a significant assignment commissioned by the International Finance Corporation, which was centered around the development of an oilseed sourcing and financing strategy. I analyzed market trends, financial flows, and sourcing mechanisms to craft a comprehensive strategy that not only met the project's objectives but also provided actionable insights and recommendations. My contributions were instrumental in shaping a strategy that facilitated efficient sourcing and financing processes, thereby supporting the broader goals of the IFC's initiative. As a result, IFC can design a financial product that benefits more than 150,000 smallholder farmers.

As a Senior Agri-Finance Specialist at the Ethiopian Agricultural Transformation Agency (ATA), I led the design and implementation of innovative financial schemes to support financial institutions and Agricultural businesses. I spearheaded partnerships with different financial institutions to facilitate asset leasing, wholesale financing, and SME credit lines. My role involved managing grants, preparing budgets, and delivering comprehensive reports to stakeholders. I provided technical assistance to 35 agribusiness companies, helping them achieve investment readiness through financial management, business planning, and ESG compliance. My leadership and communication skills were key in building strong partnerships and ensuring the success of these initiatives.

_extracted.professional_summary: Selamawit Firdissa Duresa is an accomplished gender, nutrition, and development specialist with over 20 years of professional experience in international development, agricultural transformation, humanitarian action, and organizational change management. She holds a Master of Arts in Gender Studies and a Bachelor of Arts in English Language and Literature, complemented by extensive specialized training in gender integration, food systems, agricultural development, and humanitarian response. With deep expertise in gender mainstreaming, nutrition-sensitive agriculture, youth engagement, and climate-smart approaches, she has led transformative initiatives across multiple sectors including agriculture, food security, water management, and emergency response. Her career spans roles as a gender specialist with CGIAR research institutions, senior technical expert with Ethiopia's Agricultural Transformation Institute, gender and nutrition expert with bilateral development programs, and gender advisor with humanitarian organizations. She has demonstrated capability in designing gender-sensitive strategies, building organizational capacity, developing evidence-based programming, monitoring and evaluation systems, and fostering multi-stakeholder partnerships to advance gender equality and sustainable development outcomes.

Encourage participatory ways of working with relevant stakeholders such that there is a sustained level of engagement and buy-in of the new organizational vision statement. 3. Set clear and measurable objectives of organizational change actions with milestones and check their progress on a continuous basis through self-assessment; contribute to monitoring the implementation of the change process. 4. Develop work plans to facilitate the implementation of organizational change in the relevant change areas. 5. Facilitate the development of practical proposals for organizational change in line with the vision statement and strategic framework to address the organizational issues and advise / propose mechanisms for implementation to achieve full ownership. 6. Communicate clearly at all levels during each step of the organizational change process. 7. Assist in providing both advice and concrete suggestions to the strategic management body or board of directors responsible for supporting the organizational change initiative. 8. I used to be a member of the Commission's management committee and used to take minute of the management committee meeting. Providing employee development, coaching, mentoring and team building. Tools and language used: C#, ASP.NET, VB6.0, SQL Server 2008, Visual Studio, Ajax, IIS.

Designed, developed and implemented ECX-clearing and settlement application software which was used by the exchange as a tool for clearing and settlement of daily trade transactions. Before upgrading this software, a total of more than 1 billion dollars traded with 0 defaults. This software had the functionality to link with all the clearing banks so that balance, pay-in and payout instruction will be requested from the settlement banks automatically in a secure way. The clearing module of this software calculates the total net obligation per each member and client that traded for the specified trade date. It also calculates fees and charges that the exchange deducts from each trade. The software has another section which is responsible for calculating the net obligation and settlement instruction for inventory financed commodities. Designed, developed and implemented ECX-trading application software which the trading department uses as a tool for processing trade data. Submitted trades will be verified with more than 25 trade status checks. It has a module which is optimized for fast and automatic verification of each trade which based on this verification a trade becomes accepted or rejected. The software is capable of identifying trades that does not meet the exchange's requirements. Designed and developed ECX-warehouse application. The software was used in ECX warehouses in order to sample, grade, load and create Good Receiving Notice (GRN). It has also another section for tracking Good Issue Notice (GIN) information. Maintained and enhanced the above developed software application as required by the exchange. Tools and language used: C#, VB6.0, SQL Server 2005, MS Access.

Designed and developed Decision Support system that shows the previous year's trend analysis on key educational indicators including Net Intake Rate, Gross Enrollment Rate, teachers demand, and Classroom demand. Data is extracted and analyzed using the ETL (Extract Transform and Load) system from the data warehouse. Designed, developed and implemented Enrollment Projection Model software at national level which is used in all regional education bureaus of Ethiopia. This software plays an important role in determining the future enrollment demand in the country. It will also project the associative inputs like teacher demand, classroom demand textbook demand etc. Currently, it is used as a tool in plan preparation for achieving Universal Primary Education in Ethiopia. Designed and developed Project Monitoring Software for Ministry of Education. The system was developed to monitor all projects that operate under the Ministry of Education. Both Physical Performance and financial performance of each project was monitored using this software. Designed, developed and implemented customized educational software which was used as a tool in Woreda capacity building program held in all woreda of the country. Designed, developed and implemented Material Management Information System software. MMIS is used in all regional education bureaus to manage and control their stores. It is interactive and has every feature like issue, receive, discard etc. Tools and language used: C#, ASP.NET, VB6.0, SQL Server 2005/2000.

Designed, developed school performance management software for Tigray Regional Education Bureau. This software considers all the primary schools in the region and categorizes the schools based on their performance on key educational indicators like dropout rate and repetition rate. Designed and developed Minimum Quality Standard for Access and distribution of schools software for the Regional Education Bureau. This software has been used in determining the future number of schools to be constructed taking into account School size, a radius that a student travels to the nearest school and school-aged population in the region. There are others different educational software developed during the period Jan 01-Dec 03. Such as Decision Support system that shows the previous trend analysis on key educational indicators.

employment.position: Deputy Head of Office / Project Manager for Economic and Scientific Cooperation
employment.description_of_duties:
The office represents the free state of Bavaria across four cooperation areas which are development, economic, scientific, and cultural within the five focus African countries (Ethiopia, Senegal, Kenya, Tunisia, and South Africa). Responsibilities include:
• Conceive, initiate, and develop cooperation projects in areas such as TVET support, forestry, business development, agriculture and manufacturing industry development, health, and more.
• As a representative of the financier, responsible for reviewing project proposals from potential implementers, monitoring and evaluating projects financed by the Bavarian state across the 5 focus countries, assessing their socio-economic impact, and making sure they align with project goals and efficient resource utilization.
• Consulting Bavarian SMEs interested in expanding to Africa, providing insight into regional markets.
• Creating and sending frequent reports on the development of strategic sectors such as green hydrogen and other renewables, automotive (e-mobility in particular), and finance to the Bavarian Ministry of Economic Affairs.
• Creating links between Ethiopian/ African and Bavarian Institutions (such as universities, research institutions, government offices, and CSOs) and facilitating their collaboration.
• Representing and promoting Ethiopia and Africa in economic forums and events in Bavaria such as the Bavaria-Africa forum, IFAT, and others.
• Monitoring, researching, and reporting on political and socio-economic developments across the focus countries and the African Union.
• Organization of cultural events and delegation trips to Africa by the Bavarian State Government or the Bavarian Parliament

• Consulting investors on the possible investment avenues in Ethiopia (pros and cons), and the procedures involved in licensing and registration and different services provided by relevant institutions.
• Developing business plans for enterprises in pre-implementation and expansion phases. Worked on business plans for companies in the textile, construction material manufacturing, industrial parks development, leather and real-estate sectors.
• Content manager for IPortal, an online platform designed to provide detailed information on the investment process in Ethiopia (especially for FDI). Drafted a content strategy and taken part in writing and editing over 30 articles for the website. Articles are focused on investor FAQs and the steps investors (and especially FDI) will have to go through to become operational in Ethiopia (beginning from arriving in Ethiopia).
• As content manager hired and managed a team of writers and legal analysts. Designed a training program (including introduction to investment in Ethiopia, copywriting and search engine optimization), actively monitored their progress, and managed their tasks using digital tools such as click up and slack.

• As part of the Sustainable Industrial Clusters (S.I.C.) program, tasked with developing a new manual for Industrial Parks One Stop Shop (OSS) service centers and conducting market research on available operational OSS platforms. Engaged with a lot of public and private stakeholders (EIC, ECC, MoR, CBE, CIS, Ethio-Telecom, EEU, ETIDI, IPDC, DHL and Pan Afrique Ethiopia).

• Developing comprehensive project proposals as lead researcher on behalf of TCC. Work includes proposals for a regional poultry sector development project by the Amhara regional government to develop business models and an investment case for selected intervention areas in the poultry sector.
• Drafting inception reports, designing surveys, overseeing data collection, conducting interviews and focus group discussions, coding and analysis of qualitative data. Participated in research spanning various subjects such as impact of covid on the Ethiopian MICE industry, e-governance and doing business, and constraints blocking domestic investors from entering IPs.
• Conduct feasibility studies and create investment decks.

• Develop a baseline assessment tools for the Tristate IGA project: the project is designed to provide training and funding to women from low-income families.
• Conduct a market opportunity assessment in areas the women live in and support them in setting up a business of their choice.
• Assist in the financial literacy and soft-skills training provided for the women.
• Follow up and conduct a bimonthly assessment to report on their progress and challenges they are facing.

Common tasks include providing information such as investment and industrial park laws, incentives available to them and procedural requirements at different government; and accompanying them to different institutions (such as NBE, IPDC, and EFDA) representing EIC. Worked with many of the new companies entering Kilinto Industrial Park, Adama Industrial Park and the Integrated Agro-Industrial Parks.

Through this role represented and supported Industrial Parks tenants in negotiating with different government institutions to solve their issues. INVEA, Customs Commission, Ethiopian Food and Drug Authority and Industrial Parks Development Corporation are some of the institutions regularly dealt with.

This project was aimed at mapping the history of the IP Division within EIC, its key achievements and shortcomings, challenges, mandates of the directorates within the division (as there was some clashing of mandates at the time), collaborations with other divisions and institutions and recommendations to fill the gaps.

_extracted.professional_summary: Ekram Mohammednegash Osman is an experienced development consultant and private sector development expert based in Addis Ababa, Ethiopia. As Managing Partner of Taya Consulting PLC, he provides strategic leadership and high-impact consulting services focused on employment linkage, labour market development, private sector growth, and gender and social inclusion. With over a decade of professional experience spanning from academic lecturing to programme management, Ekram has demonstrated expertise in designing and implementing interventions in areas including women's economic empowerment, access to finance, business development services, psychosocial support, and youth employability. His work has involved managing complex multi-stakeholder programmes funded by major foundations including the MasterCard Foundation and GIZ, working across industrial parks and special economic zones in Ethiopia and Rwanda. He combines technical knowledge in monitoring and evaluation, value chain analysis, and qualitative research with a strong commitment to gender equality and social inclusion across all development initiatives.

employment.description_of_duties: Oversee the strategic direction and operational management of Taya Consulting PLC, and develop and maintain client relationships. Coordinate the training and recruitment of talents and provide strategic oversight for program outcomes.

employment.position: Gender Youth and Social Inclusion and Psychosocial Support Lead
employment.description_of_duties: Led a team on MESMER program which is a program funded by MasterCard Foundation and is implemented by First Consult PLC. The MESMER program works on putting in place an access to finance, business development, and psychosocial services to help MSMEs recover from shocks, build resilience and grow to create dignified and fulfilling work for the youth. From the design of the proposal phase involved in the implementation of the 4 components mainly leading the Psychosocial and the Business development of MESMER program. Interventions within MESMER are focused on putting a system for MSMEs recover from shocks and bridge the financial gap for MSMEs by expanding the availability of affordable credit and enhancing the creditworthiness of MSMEs through BDS, Psychosocial support (PSS), and TA to financial institutions. Developed a gender strategy for MESMER program in order to actualize the reach of young women target (80%) for the coming 5 years. Mainstreaming gender and inclusion aspect of the program within the four components and project activities address gender equality, women's empowerment and positive youth development. Provide technical and managerial oversight to all the team and regional staffs on the access to finance aspect of grant and credit, PSS and BDS along with gender and youth specialists based in all regions the program covers. Ensure that gender and youth is integrated and mainstreamed across all value chains which are selected for the project. Conduct studies, assessment, and document to identify challenges and opportunities in the program and prepare a strategy and recommendation to address the challenges. Focus on the conflict affected regions to avail skills and promoting resilience in MSMEs to recover from and adapt to the effects of conflict through a robust PSS delivery and make MSMEs enhance their life skills to be productive in running their businesses. Design and implement PSS intervention like group and individual counselling for MSMEs to bounce back from the collapse of markets, the breaking of supply chains and the depletion of workforce capacity. Target 80% women with special women economic empowerment capacity building which also encompasses reproductive health and gender-based violence trainings.

BRIDGES is a Mastercard Foundation funded program which focuses on job creation and private sector development program working on youth employability skills mainly on Special Economic Zone (SEZ) areas. Being part of the management team and involved from its proposal design phase, was part of the investment committee to approve concept notes on new and innovative intervention and budget for the intervention presented from all BRIDGES team. By implementing the IP recruitment database system, the BRIDGES program was able to present a systemic and informative tool for policy makers to have access to predictable and reliable labour market information and products. The private sector was also able to rely on the system which facilitated and eased the recruitment system in the industrial parks. Additionally, was able to design and implement a targeted retention mechanism within factories through the development of soft skill training programs and curriculum aimed at easing the transition of workers into the Industrial workforce. As 95% of the beneficiaries are women, the soft skill training curriculum was designed from gender sensitive which is focused on gender equality and empowerment for women. Developed a policy briefing with recommendation on the expulsion of Ethiopia from AGOA and the economic contribution of foreign direct investment and Industrial Parks to Ethiopia. Held a workshop with Ethiopian Investment Commission management and other stakeholders and discussed the way forward. Working closely with Industrial Park Development Corporation (IPDC) developed an affordable housing document that identify the issues that hinder the implementation of past studies on affordable housing and attempts at developing housing for industrial park workers in Ethiopia where it also proposes a workable solution. The document was presented to all stakeholders in order to inform the policy that IPDC was drafting on affordable housings for IP workers. Closely work Stakeholder, like the private sector both FDI and local, government and other donor programs, designed and implemented the efficiency of the worker recruitment platform for industrial Parks across Ethiopia. By addressing quantitative and information mismatch issues in the recruitment process of the Industrial Park labour database aids efficient employment matching and tracks trends in: IP-specific key labour metrics; labour demand and supply; turnover; and demographic information.
